title: /kernel
summary_text: Kernel bootstrap failed closed. This repo’s `/kernel` contract requires `6+` live subagent lanes before Kernel mode is considered valid. The static contract check passed with `bash tests/test-codex-kernel-prompt.sh`, but every spawned lane hit the same `GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark` usage limit, so the runtime minimum could not be sustained. The provider error said retry is available at `March 13, 2026 9:23 AM`. Main-thread findings: - Kernel here is the Codex-first sovereign successor to FUGUE, with the primary doctrine in [CODEX.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/CODEX.md), [requirements-gpt54-codex-kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/docs/requirements-gpt54-codex-kernel.md), and [kernel-structure.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/docs/kernel-structure.md). - Readiness is documented as “substantially met,” but the unattended runtime substrate and some adapter/runtime promotions are still incomplete in [kernel-preimplementation-readiness.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/docs/kernel-preimplementation-readiness.md). If you want to proceed right now, say explicitly to waive Kernel mode and I’ll continue in normal single-thread Codex mode. Otherwise, rerun `/kernel` after the lane capacity recovers.
